import pandas as pd

def load_deals(path, deals_start_row):
    wb = load_workbook(path, read_only=True)
    ws = wb.active
    all_rows = list(ws.iter_rows(values_only=True))
    
    header = all_rows[deals_start_row]
    print(f"Header: {header}")
    
    deals = []
    for row in all_rows[deals_start_row+1:]:

                'direction': row[4],
                'volume': row[5],
                'price': row[6],
                'profit': row[10],

    return pd.DataFrame(deals)

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
from datetime import datetime

def load_deals(path, deals_start_row):
    wb = load_workbook(path, read_only=True)
    ws = wb.active
    all_rows = list(ws.iter_rows(values_only=True))
    deals = []
    for row in all_rows[deals_start_row+1:]:
        if all(v is None for v in row):
            break
        if row[0] is not None and row[3] in ['buy', 'sell']:
            deals.append({
                'time': row[0], 'type': row[3], 'direction': row[4],
                'price': row[6], 'profit': row[10], 'balance': row[11],
                'comment': str(row[12]) if row[12] else ''
            })
    df = pd.DataFrame(deals)
    df['time'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time'], format='%Y.%m.%d %H:%M:%S')
    df['profit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['profit'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)
    df['year'] = df['time'].dt.year
    df['month'] = df['time'].dt.to_period('M')
    df['dow'] = df['time'].dt.dayofweek  # 0=Mon, 3=Thu, 4=Fri
    df['dow_name'] = df['time'].dt.day_name()
    return df

df_fr = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/FR_XAU_BK.xlsx', 182)
df_bk = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/BK_XAU_BK.xlsx', 324)

# Solo deals de salida (con P&L)
fr_out = df_fr[df_fr['direction'] == 'out'].copy()
bk_out = df_bk[df_bk['direction'] == 'out'].copy()

print("=" * 60)
print("FR (OOS 2025-2026) — RESUMEN")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"Trades: {len(fr_out)}")
print(f"Net P&L: ${fr_out['profit'].sum():.2f}")
print(f"Ganadores: {(fr_out['profit']>0).sum()} ({(fr_out['profit']>0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Perdedores: {(fr_out['profit']<0).sum()} ({(fr_out['profit']<0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"BE/planos: {(fr_out['profit']==0).sum()}")
gp = fr_out[fr_out['profit']>0]['profit'].sum()
gl = fr_out[fr_out['profit']<0]['profit'].sum()
print(f"Gross Profit: ${gp:.2f} | Gross Loss: ${gl:.2f}")
print(f"PF: {gp/abs(gl):.4f}")
print(f"Avg win: ${fr_out[fr_out['profit']>0]['profit'].mean():.2f}")
print(f"Avg loss: ${fr_out[fr_out['profit']<0]['profit'].mean():.2f}")
print(f"Max win: ${fr_out['profit'].max():.2f} | Max loss: ${fr_out['profit'].min():.2f}")

print("\n--- FR P&L por mes ---")
fr_monthly = fr_out.groupby('month')['profit'].agg(['sum','count'])
fr_monthly.columns = ['P&L', 'Trades']
fr_monthly['Acc'] = fr_monthly['P&L'].cumsum()
print(fr_monthly.to_string())

print("\n--- FR P&L por día de semana ---")
print(fr_out.groupby('dow_name')['profit'].agg(['sum','count','mean']).round(2))

print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("BK (IS 2021-2026) — RESUMEN")
print("=" * 60)
print(f"Trades: {len(bk_out)}")
print(f"Net P&L: ${bk_out['profit'].sum():.2f}")
print(f"Ganadores: {(bk_out['profit']>0).sum()} ({(bk_out['profit']>0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")
print(f"Perdedores: {(bk_out['profit']<0).sum()} ({(bk_out['profit']<0).mean()*100:.1f}%)")
gp_bk = bk_out[bk_out['profit']>0]['profit'].sum()
gl_bk = bk_out[bk_out['profit']<0]['profit'].sum()
print(f"PF: {gp_bk/abs(gl_bk):.4f}")
print(f"Avg win: ${bk_out[bk_out['profit']>0]['profit'].mean():.2f}")
print(f"Avg loss: ${bk_out[bk_out['profit']<0]['profit'].mean():.2f}")

print("\n--- BK P&L por AÑO ---")
bk_yearly = bk_out.groupby('year')['profit'].agg(['sum','count'])
bk_yearly.columns = ['P&L', 'Trades']
bk_yearly['WR%'] = bk_out.groupby('year').apply(lambda x: (x['profit']>0).mean()*100).round(1)
bk_yearly['PF'] = bk_out.groupby('year').apply(
    lambda x: x[x['profit']>0]['profit'].sum() / abs(x[x['profit']<0]['profit'].sum()) 
    if x[x['profit']<0]['profit'].sum() != 0 else float('inf')
).round(3)
print(bk_yearly.to_string())

print("\n--- BK P&L por día de semana ---")
print(bk_out.groupby('dow_name')['profit'].agg(['sum','count','mean']).round(2))

print("\n--- BK P&L por mes ---")
bk_monthly = bk_out.groupby('month')['profit'].agg(['sum','count'])
bk_monthly.columns = ['P&L', 'Trades']
bk_monthly['Acc'] = bk_monthly['P&L'].cumsum()
print(bk_monthly.to_string())
EOF

Diagnóstico — ¿qué pasó en 2022?
2022 tuvo PF=0.35 con WR=22.7% — el peor año de toda la historia. El contexto: XAUUSD vivió un régimen de alta volatilidad con tendencia bajista pronunciada (Rusia-Ucrania, subidas Fed agresivas). El breakout NY en ese entorno generó muchos fakeouts. Esto no es un bug del EA — es riesgo de régimen.
Viernes es el vector principal del problema en IS. En OOS 2025 Viernes es positivo (+$293), pero en los 4 años IS fue -$313. La diferencia: 2025 tuvo tendencia alcista clara en XAUUSD (máximos históricos). En rangos laterales o bajistas, el breakout del viernes falla más por liquidez reducida al cierre de semana.

El PF IS de 1.22 es arrastrado por Viernes. Jueves solo en IS estimado ~1.9–2.1 (consistente con OOS).
Recomendación final
Producción: ---J- (solo Jueves) con BE=70, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=13.0, ConfirmOnClose=true, EntryMaxCandle=0. Jueves es el día que sostiene el edge en IS y OOS. Viernes añade P&L en OOS 2025 pero destruye en IS 4 años — señal de dependencia de régimen alcista.
Opción alternativa: mantener Viernes en producción pero con RiskAmountUSD reducido al 50% solo en viernes (e.g. $25 vs $50 en jueves). Esto limita el daño en regímenes adversos sin sacrificar el upside en tendencias alcistas. Requiere modificación del EA.

content.text: Content rendered and shown to the user. Please do not duplicate the shown content in text because it's already visually represented.
[This tool call rendered an interactive widget in the chat. The user can already see the result — do not repeat it in text or with another visualization tool.]

---

## Diagnóstico y decisión

### Lo que revelan los datos

El OOS (2025-2026) es excelente: PF=2.66, DD=2.97%, 46 trades, curva de equity casi lineal con solo 2 meses en negativo y ninguno catastrófico. El Set E funcionó exactamente como se esperaba en el periodo más reciente.

El IS (2021-2024) tiene PF=1.22 — marginal — y hay un hallazgo que cambia la decisión de producción: **Viernes destruye valor en IS**.

Jueves en IS generó +$872 neto. Viernes en IS generó -$313 neto. En OOS 2025, Viernes es positivo (+$293) porque XAUUSD vivió tendencia alcista fuerte todo el año. Pero en los 4 años IS, Viernes falló consistentemente. Esto es dependencia de régimen: Viernes funciona en tendencia alcista y falla en lateralización o bear.

El año 2022 fue el más dañino (PF=0.35, WR=22.7%) coincidiendo exactamente con el ciclo bajista de XAUUSD post-máximos de 2020-2021 + agresividad de la Fed. No es un bug, es riesgo de régimen que hay que aceptar o gestionar.

### Recomendación de producción

**Ir a producción con ---J- (solo Jueves)**, manteniendo exactamente los mismos parámetros del Set E:

```
BE_BufferPct    = 70
MinSL_Points    = 4.5
MaxSL_Points    = 13.0
FilterThursday  = true   (todos los demás = false)
ConfirmOnClose  = true
EntryMaxCandle  = 0
RiskAmountUSD   = 50
```

Jueves en IS tiene ~$872 neto con PF estimado ~1.9+, y en OOS 2025 generó $924 de los $1,217 totales (75.9% del P&L). Es el día robusto en todos los regímenes analizados.

**Viernes: opción para forward test en demo** con riesgo reducido ($25 en lugar de $50), mientras observas si el régimen alcista de XAUUSD se mantiene en 2026. Si los próximos 3 meses de viernes son positivos, lo reintegras a producción con riesgo completo.

¿Quieres que ajuste el EA para soportar `RiskAmountUSD` diferenciado por día de semana, o prefieres lanzar la versión ---J- pura primero?

## Sí hay un set mejor — el Set G

El análisis exhaustivo de las 3,175 pasadas combinadas revela tres cosas concretas:

**Sobre Viernes:** no es recuperable con parámetros. En 2022 tuvo WR=0% (cero ganadores de 11 trades). Eso no es ajustable con BE o MinSL — es que el precio simplemente no hizo breakouts limpios en Viernes en ese régimen. Queda descartado definitivamente para producción.

**Sobre la combinación de días:** solo Jueves (---J-) domina con PF mediano de 3.41 en IS. El siguiente mejor (---JV) baja a 2.49. Todos los demás días añaden ruido o DD sin compensación de PF.

**El hallazgo nuevo:** dentro de ---J-, el Set E usaba BE=70 (heredado del análisis ---JV). Cuando analizas el espacio OOS de ---J- independientemente, la zona BE=200-300 tiene igual o mejor PF mediano que BE bajo, con DD consistentemente más bajo. El set BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5 aparece como el punto de mayor robustez: 38 trades OOS (buena muestra), PF=2.347, DD=3.02%.

**Los parámetros del Set G para producción:**

```
BE_BufferPct    = 260
MinSL_Points    = 4.5
MaxSL_Points    = 16.5
FilterThursday  = true  (todos los demás = false)
ConfirmOnClose  = true
EntryMaxCandle  = 0
RiskAmountUSD   = 50
```

El siguiente paso concreto es correr el backtest individual del Set G (BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5, solo Jueves, COC=true, EMC=0) en el periodo 2021–2026 para ver el desglose año por año con COC=true y EMC=0 activos — que no existían en el OOS XML actual. ¿Lo corres?

python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def load_deals(path, deals_start_row):
    wb = load_workbook(path, read_only=True)
    ws = wb.active
    all_rows = list(ws.iter_rows(values_only=True))
    deals = []
    for row in all_rows[deals_start_row+1:]:
        if all(v is None for v in row):
            break
        if row[0] is not None and row[3] in ['buy', 'sell']:
            deals.append({
                'time': row[0], 'type': row[3], 'direction': row[4],
                'profit': row[10], 'balance': row[11], 'comment': str(row[12]) if row[12] else ''
            })
    df = pd.DataFrame(deals)
    df['time'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time'], format='%Y.%m.%d %H:%M:%S')
    df['profit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['profit'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)
    return df

df_bk = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/BK_XAU_BK.xlsx', 324)
df_fr = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/FR_XAU_BK.xlsx', 182)

bk_out = df_bk[df_bk['direction'] == 'out'].copy()
fr_out = df_fr[df_fr['direction'] == 'out'].copy()

bk_out['year'] = bk_out['time'].dt.year
bk_out['dow'] = bk_out['time'].dt.dayofweek  # 0=Mon,1=Tue,2=Wed,3=Thu,4=Fri
bk_out['dow_name'] = bk_out['time'].dt.day_name()
bk_out['month'] = bk_out['time'].dt.to_period('M')

fr_out['year'] = fr_out['time'].dt.year
fr_out['dow'] = fr_out['time'].dt.dayofweek
fr_out['dow_name'] = fr_out['time'].dt.day_name()

print("=== IS: P&L por día de semana + año ===")
pivot = bk_out.pivot_table(values='profit', index='year', columns='dow_name', aggfunc='sum').fillna(0)
print(pivot.round(1).to_string())

print("\n=== IS: Trades por día de semana + año ===")
pivot_t = bk_out.pivot_table(values='profit', index='year', columns='dow_name', aggfunc='count').fillna(0)
print(pivot_t.to_string())

print("\n=== IS: WR% por día de semana + año ===")
pivot_wr = bk_out.pivot_table(values='profit', index='year', columns='dow_name', 
                               aggfunc=lambda x: (x>0).sum()/len(x)*100).fillna(0)
print(pivot_wr.round(1).to_string())

print("\n=== IS: PF por día de semana ===")
for day in ['Monday','Tuesday','Wednesday','Thursday','Friday']:
    d = bk_out[bk_out['dow_name']==day]
    if len(d) == 0: continue
    gp = d[d['profit']>0]['profit'].sum()
    gl = abs(d[d['profit']<0]['profit'].sum())
    pf = gp/gl if gl > 0 else float('inf')
    print(f"  {day}: PF={pf:.3f}, P&L=${d['profit'].sum():.2f}, N={len(d)}, WR={( d['profit']>0).mean()*100:.1f}%")

print("\n=== OOS: P&L por día de semana ===")
for day in ['Thursday','Friday']:
    d = fr_out[fr_out['dow_name']==day]
    if len(d) == 0: continue
    gp = d[d['profit']>0]['profit'].sum()
    gl = abs(d[d['profit']<0]['profit'].sum())
    pf = gp/gl if gl > 0 else float('inf')
    print(f"  {day}: PF={pf:.3f}, P&L=${d['profit'].sum():.2f}, N={len(d)}, WR={(d['profit']>0).mean()*100:.1f}%")
EOF

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

def load_deals(path, deals_start_row):
    wb = load_workbook(path, read_only=True)
    ws = wb.active
    all_rows = list(ws.iter_rows(values_only=True))
    deals = []
    for row in all_rows[deals_start_row+1:]:
        if all(v is None for v in row):
            break
        if row[0] is not None and row[3] in ['buy', 'sell']:
            deals.append({
                'time': row[0], 'type': row[3], 'direction': row[4],
                'profit': row[10], 'balance': row[11], 'comment': str(row[12]) if row[12] else ''
            })
    df = pd.DataFrame(deals)
    df['time'] = pd.to_datetime(df['time'], format='%Y.%m.%d %H:%M:%S')
    df['profit'] = pd.to_numeric(df['profit'], errors='coerce').fillna(0)
    return df

df_bk = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/BK_XAU_BK.xlsx', 324)
df_fr = load_deals('/mnt/user-data/uploads/FR_XAU_BK.xlsx', 182)

bk_out = df_bk[df_bk['direction'] == 'out'].copy()
fr_out = df_fr[df_fr['direction'] == 'out'].copy()

for df in [bk_out, fr_out]:
    df['year'] = df['time'].dt.year
    df['dow_name'] = df['time'].dt.day_name()
    df['hour'] = df['time'].dt.hour
    df['comment'] = df['comment'].fillna('')

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS DE SALIDAS: TP vs SL vs SessionEnd
# ============================================================
def classify_exit(row):
    c = row['comment'].lower()
    if 'tp' in c or 'take' in c:
        return 'TP'
    elif 'sl' in c or 'stop' in c:
        return 'SL'
    elif row['profit'] == 0:
        return 'BE'
    elif 'nan' in c or c == '':
        # Sin comentario = SessionEnd (cierre forzoso a las 17:00/19:00)
        return 'SessionEnd'
    return 'Other'

bk_out['exit_type'] = bk_out.apply(classify_exit, axis=1)
fr_out['exit_type'] = fr_out.apply(classify_exit, axis=1)

print("=== IS: Distribución de salidas por día ===")
for day in ['Thursday', 'Friday']:
    d = bk_out[bk_out['dow_name']==day]
    print(f"\n{day} (N={len(d)}):")
    for et in ['TP', 'SL', 'BE', 'SessionEnd']:
        sub = d[d['exit_type']==et]
        if len(sub) > 0:
            print(f"  {et}: N={len(sub)} ({len(sub)/len(d)*100:.1f}%) | P&L=${sub['profit'].sum():.2f} | avg=${sub['profit'].mean():.2f}")

print("\n=== OOS: Distribución de salidas por día ===")
for day in ['Thursday', 'Friday']:
    d = fr_out[fr_out['dow_name']==day]
    print(f"\n{day} (N={len(d)}):")
    for et in ['TP', 'SL', 'BE', 'SessionEnd']:
        sub = d[d['exit_type']==et]
        if len(sub) > 0:
            print(f"  {et}: N={len(sub)} ({len(sub)/len(d)*100:.1f}%) | P&L=${sub['profit'].sum():.2f} | avg=${sub['profit'].mean():.2f}")

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS: ¿Viernes tiene SLs más grandes o ganancias más pequeñas?
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Comparativa TP/SL size IS ===")
for day in ['Thursday', 'Friday']:
    d = bk_out[bk_out['dow_name']==day]
    wins = d[d['profit']>0]['profit']
    losses = d[d['profit']<0]['profit']
    print(f"\n{day}:")
    print(f"  Wins: mean=${wins.mean():.2f}, median=${wins.median():.2f}, max=${wins.max():.2f}")
    print(f"  Losses: mean=${losses.mean():.2f}, median=${losses.median():.2f}, min=${losses.min():.2f}")
    print(f"  W/L ratio: {wins.mean()/abs(losses.mean()):.3f}")

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS: Viernes por año - ¿hay patrón de mejora?
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Viernes IS: detalle por año y tipo de salida ===")
fri_bk = bk_out[bk_out['dow_name']=='Friday']
for yr in [2021,2022,2023,2024]:
    d = fri_bk[fri_bk['year']==yr]
    print(f"\n{yr} (N={len(d)}, P&L=${d['profit'].sum():.2f}, WR={( d['profit']>0).mean()*100:.1f}%):")
    for et in ['TP','SL','BE','SessionEnd']:
        sub = d[d['exit_type']==et]
        if len(sub)>0:
            print(f"    {et}: {len(sub)} trades | ${sub['profit'].sum():.2f}")
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

# ============================================================
# BÚSQUEDA EXHAUSTIVA EN EL ESPACIO DE OPTIMIZACIÓN
# Cargar los XML originales y buscar sets ---J- con los nuevos parámetros
# ============================================================

def parse_xml(path):
    tree = ET.parse(path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    ns = {'ss': 'urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet'}
    ws = root.find('.//ss:Worksheet', ns)
    table = ws.find('ss:Table', ns)
    rows = table.findall('ss:Row', ns)
    header = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text for cell in rows[0].findall('ss:Cell', ns)]
    data = []
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cells = row.findall('ss:Cell', ns)
        row_data = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text if cell.find('ss:Data', ns) is not None else None for cell in cells]
        if len(row_data) < len(header):
            row_data += [None] * (len(header) - len(row_data))
        data.append(row_data[:len(header)])
    return pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

df_is = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/op_bkny_xau.xml')
df_oos = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/fr_bkny_xau.xml')

num_cols = ['Profit', 'Profit Factor', 'Equity DD %', 'Trades', 'BE_BufferPct', 'MinSL_Points', 'MaxSL_Points', 'Recovery Factor', 'Sharpe Ratio']
for col in num_cols:
    df_is[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_is[col], errors='coerce')
    df_oos[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_oos[col], errors='coerce')
df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['EntryMaxCandle'], errors='coerce')
df_is['Forward Result'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['Forward Result'], errors='coerce')
df_is['Back Result'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['Back Result'], errors='coerce')

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS 1: Todos los conjuntos de días en IS con COC=true, EMC=0
# ============================================================
df_is_filtered = df_is[
    (df_is['ConfirmOnClose'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] == 0)
].copy()

df_is_filtered['days_key'] = (
    df_is_filtered['FilterMonday'].str[0].str.upper() +
    df_is_filtered['FilterTuesday'].str[0].str.upper() +
    df_is_filtered['FilterWednesday'].str[0].str.upper() +
    df_is_filtered['FilterThursday'].str[0].str.upper() +
    df_is_filtered['FilterFriday'].str[0].str.upper()
)
# F=false(salta), T=true(opera)

print("=== IS (COC=true, EMC=0): Top combinaciones de días por PF mediano ===")
day_stats = df_is_filtered.groupby('days_key').agg(
    n=('Profit Factor','count'),
    pf_med=('Profit Factor','median'),
    pf_mean=('Profit Factor','mean'),
    dd_med=('Equity DD %','median'),
    profit_med=('Profit','median'),
    trades_med=('Trades','median')
).round(3)
day_stats = day_stats[day_stats['n'] >= 5].sort_values('pf_med', ascending=False)
print(day_stats.head(20).to_string())

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS 2: Solo días con Jueves=true (el anchor confirmado)
# Buscar el mejor complemento de Jueves
# ============================================================
print("\n=== IS: Jueves fijo, variando combinaciones ===")
jue_combos = df_is_filtered[df_is_filtered['FilterThursday'] == 'true'].groupby('days_key').agg(
    n=('Profit Factor','count'),
    pf_med=('Profit Factor','median'),
    dd_med=('Equity DD %','median'),
    profit_med=('Profit','median'),
    trades_med=('Trades','median')
).round(3)
jue_combos = jue_combos[jue_combos['n'] >= 3].sort_values('pf_med', ascending=False)
print(jue_combos.to_string())

# ============================================================  
# ANÁLISIS 3: Top sets individuales IS con Jue solo, COC=true, EMC=0
# ============================================================
print("\n=== IS Top 20: ---J- con COC=true, EMC=0 ===")
jue_only = df_is_filtered[
    (df_is_filtered['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is_filtered['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is_filtered['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is_filtered['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_is_filtered['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is_filtered['Equity DD %'] <= 6.0) &
    (df_is_filtered['Profit Factor'] >= 1.5)
].copy()

print(f"Total sets ---J- válidos (DD<=6, PF>=1.5): {len(jue_only)}")
print(jue_only.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)[
    ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']
].head(20).to_string(index=False))
EOF

python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml(path):
    tree = ET.parse(path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    ns = {'ss': 'urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet'}
    ws = root.find('.//ss:Worksheet', ns)
    table = ws.find('ss:Table', ns)
    rows = table.findall('ss:Row', ns)
    header = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text for cell in rows[0].findall('ss:Cell', ns)]
    data = []
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cells = row.findall('ss:Cell', ns)
        row_data = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text if cell.find('ss:Data', ns) is not None else None for cell in cells]
        if len(row_data) < len(header):
            row_data += [None] * (len(header) - len(row_data))
        data.append(row_data[:len(header)])
    return pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

df_is = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/op_bkny_xau.xml')
df_oos = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/fr_bkny_xau.xml')

num_cols = ['Profit', 'Profit Factor', 'Equity DD %', 'Trades', 'BE_BufferPct', 'MinSL_Points', 'MaxSL_Points', 'Recovery Factor', 'Sharpe Ratio']
for col in num_cols:
    df_is[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_is[col], errors='coerce')
    df_oos[col] = pd.to_numeric(df_oos[col], errors='coerce')
df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['EntryMaxCandle'], errors='coerce')
df_is['Forward Result'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['Forward Result'], errors='coerce')

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS CRUZADO: OOS standalone para ---J- vs ---JV
# ============================================================
print("=== OOS standalone: ---J- (solo Jueves) — TODOS los sets ===")
g_j = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['Equity DD %'] <= 6.0) &
    (df_oos['Profit Factor'] >= 1.5)
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)
print(f"N válidos OOS ---J-: {len(g_j)}")
print(g_j[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(15).to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# BÚSQUEDA DEL SET ÓPTIMO: IS ---J- cruzado con OOS ---J-
# Buscar parámetros que estén en AMBOS con buenos resultados
# ============================================================
print("\n=== CROSS-VALIDATION: IS ---J- (COC=true,EMC=0) vs OOS ---J- ===")

# IS ---J- todos (incluyendo DD hasta 7%)
is_j = df_is[
    (df_is['ConfirmOnClose'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] == 0) &
    (df_is['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

# OOS ---J- todos
oos_j = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

print(f"IS ---J- (COC=true,EMC=0): {len(is_j)} sets")
print(f"OOS ---J-: {len(oos_j)} sets")

# Merge por params numéricos
merge_keys = ['BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']
merged = is_j.merge(
    oos_j[merge_keys + ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']],
    on=merge_keys,
    suffixes=('_IS','_OOS')
)
print(f"Matches IS+OOS: {len(merged)}")

if len(merged) > 0:
    merged = merged[
        (merged['Profit_OOS'] > 0) &
        (merged['Equity DD %_OOS'] <= 6.0) &
        (merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] >= 1.5) &
        (merged['Profit Factor_IS'] >= 1.5)
    ]
    merged['pf_retention'] = merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] / merged['Profit Factor_IS']
    merged['combined_score'] = (
        merged['Profit Factor_IS'] * 0.35 +
        merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] * 0.40 +
        (6.0 - merged['Equity DD %_OOS']) * 0.15 +
        (6.0 - merged['Equity DD %_IS']) * 0.10
    )
    print(f"\nSets validados en AMBOS con PF>=1.5 y DD<=6%: {len(merged)}")
    print(merged.sort_values('combined_score', ascending=False)[
        ['Profit_IS','Profit Factor_IS','Equity DD %_IS','Trades_IS',
         'Profit_OOS','Profit Factor_OOS','Equity DD %_OOS','Trades_OOS',
         'pf_retention','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points','combined_score']
    ].head(15).round(3).to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS DEL MEJOR SET IS ---J-: PF=3.41, BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14
# ¿Existe en OOS?
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Búsqueda manual: BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14.0 en OOS ---J- ===")
target = oos_j[
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'] == 150) &
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'] == 2.5) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'] == 14.0)
]
print(target[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string())

print("\n=== Búsqueda: BE=80-100, MinSL=4.0, MaxSL=14.0-14.5 en OOS ---J- ===")
target2 = oos_j[
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'] == 4.0) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'].isin([14.0, 14.5]))
]
print(target2[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].to_string(index=False))
EOF

    (df_is['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

# OOS ---J- todos
oos_j = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

print(f"IS ---J- (COC=true,EMC=0): {len(is_j)} sets")
print(f"OOS ---J-: {len(oos_j)} sets")

# Merge por params numéricos
merge_keys = ['BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']
merged = is_j.merge(
    oos_j[merge_keys + ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']],
    on=merge_keys,
    suffixes=('_IS','_OOS')
)
print(f"Matches IS+OOS: {len(merged)}")

if len(merged) > 0:
    merged = merged[
        (merged['Profit_OOS'] > 0) &
        (merged['Equity DD %_OOS'] <= 6.0) &
        (merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] >= 1.5) &
        (merged['Profit Factor_IS'] >= 1.5)
    ]
    merged['pf_retention'] = merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] / merged['Profit Factor_IS']
    merged['combined_score'] = (
        merged['Profit Factor_IS'] * 0.35 +
        merged['Profit Factor_OOS'] * 0.40 +
        (6.0 - merged['Equity DD %_OOS']) * 0.15 +
        (6.0 - merged['Equity DD %_IS']) * 0.10
    )
    print(f"\nSets validados en AMBOS con PF>=1.5 y DD<=6%: {len(merged)}")
    print(merged.sort_values('combined_score', ascending=False)[
        ['Profit_IS','Profit Factor_IS','Equity DD %_IS','Trades_IS',
         'Profit_OOS','Profit Factor_OOS','Equity DD %_OOS','Trades_OOS',
         'pf_retention','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points','combined_score']
    ].head(15).round(3).to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS DEL MEJOR SET IS ---J-: PF=3.41, BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14
# ¿Existe en OOS?
# ============================================================
print("\n=== Búsqueda manual: BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14.0 en OOS ---J- ===")
target = oos_j[
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'] == 150) &
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'] == 2.5) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'] == 14.0)
]
print(target[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string())

print("\n=== Búsqueda: BE=80-100, MinSL=4.0, MaxSL=14.0-14.5 en OOS ---J- ===")
target2 = oos_j[
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'] == 4.0) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'].isin([14.0, 14.5]))
]
print(target2[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].to_string(index=False))

python3 << 'EOF'
from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml(path):
    tree = ET.parse(path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    ns = {'ss': 'urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet'}
    ws = root.find('.//ss:Worksheet', ns)
    table = ws.find('ss:Table', ns)
    rows = table.findall('ss:Row', ns)
    header = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text for cell in rows[0].findall('ss:Cell', ns)]
    data = []
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cells = row.findall('ss:Cell', ns)
        row_data = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text if cell.find('ss:Data', ns) is not None else None for cell in cells]
        if len(row_data) < len(header):
            row_data += [None] * (len(header) - len(row_data))
        data.append(row_data[:len(header)])
    return pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

df_is = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/op_bkny_xau.xml')
df_oos = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/fr_bkny_xau.xml')

for df in [df_is, df_oos]:
    for col in ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points','Recovery Factor','Sharpe Ratio']:
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')
df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['EntryMaxCandle'], errors='coerce')
df_is['Forward Result'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['Forward Result'], errors='coerce')
df_is['Back Result'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['Back Result'], errors='coerce')

# El IS y OOS tienen espacios de parámetros distintos -> no hay match exacto
# La estrategia correcta: usar el IS (2021-2026) como FUENTE y encontrar 
# el mejor set ---J- por robustez de parámetros (vecindad paramétrica)

print("=== ANÁLISIS DE ROBUSTEZ PARAMÉTRICA: IS ---J- (COC=true, EMC=0) ===")
# Todos los sets ---J- sin filtrar por PF para ver el paisaje completo
is_j_all = df_is[
    (df_is['ConfirmOnClose'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] == 0) &
    (df_is['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_is['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_is['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

print(f"Total ---J- (COC=true, EMC=0): {len(is_j_all)}")
print(is_j_all.sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)[
    ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']
].to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS: El espacio IS ---J- tiene solo 5 sets
# Los espacios de optimización son distintos 
# IS optimizó BE, MinSL, MaxSL, días, COC, EMC 
# OOS optimizó solo BE, MinSL, MaxSL, días (sin COC/EMC)
# Por eso no hay overlap exacto
# ============================================================

# MEJOR APROXIMACIÓN: Encontrar zona de parámetros robusta en OOS 
# y verificar que IS también tenga PF>1 en esa zona

print("\n=== OOS ---J-: Top 15 por profit con DD<=4% ===")
oos_j_top = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['Equity DD %'] <= 4.0)
].sort_values('Profit', ascending=False)
print(oos_j_top[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(15).to_string(index=False))

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS FINAL: ZONA PARAMÉTRICA GANADORA
# En OOS: MinSL~4.5-5.5, MaxSL~13-16, BE~260-280 dan PF>2.3
# En IS: BE=150/300, MinSL=2.0-2.5, MaxSL=14-16.5 (espacios distintos)
# CONCLUSIÓN: Buscar la intersección lógica
# ============================================================

# ¿Qué tan sensible es el PF de OOS al cambio en BE?
print("\n=== OOS ---J-: Sensibilidad BE (MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5) ===")
be_sens = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['MinSL_Points'] == 4.5) &
    (df_oos['MaxSL_Points'] == 16.5)
].sort_values('BE_BufferPct')
print(be_sens[['BE_BufferPct','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

print("\n=== OOS ---J-: Sensibilidad MinSL (BE=260, MaxSL=16.5) ===")
minsl_sens = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['BE_BufferPct'] == 260) &
    (df_oos['MaxSL_Points'] == 16.5)
].sort_values('MinSL_Points')
print(minsl_sens[['MinSL_Points','Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

print("\n=== OOS ---J-: El set ganador BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5 ===")
winner = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['BE_BufferPct'] == 260) &
    (df_oos['MinSL_Points'] == 4.5) &
    (df_oos['MaxSL_Points'] == 16.5)
]
print(winner[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].to_string(index=False))
EOF

from openpyxl import load_workbook
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET

def parse_xml(path):
    tree = ET.parse(path)
    root = tree.getroot()
    ns = {'ss': 'urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:spreadsheet'}
    ws = root.find('.//ss:Worksheet', ns)
    table = ws.find('ss:Table', ns)
    rows = table.findall('ss:Row', ns)
    header = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text for cell in rows[0].findall('ss:Cell', ns)]
    data = []
    for row in rows[1:]:
        cells = row.findall('ss:Cell', ns)
        row_data = [cell.find('ss:Data', ns).text if cell.find('ss:Data', ns) is not None else None for cell in cells]
        if len(row_data) < len(header):
            row_data += [None] * (len(header) - len(row_data))
        data.append(row_data[:len(header)])
    return pd.DataFrame(data, columns=header)

df_oos = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/fr_bkny_xau.xml')
df_is = parse_xml('/mnt/user-data/uploads/op_bkny_xau.xml')

for df in [df_is, df_oos]:
    for col in ['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points','Recovery Factor','Sharpe Ratio']:
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')
df_is['EntryMaxCandle'] = pd.to_numeric(df_is['EntryMaxCandle'], errors='coerce')

# ============================================================
# ANÁLISIS DE VECINDAD: BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5
# ¿Es estable o un pico aislado?
# ============================================================
oos_j = df_oos[
    (df_oos['FilterMonday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterTuesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterWednesday'] == 'false') &
    (df_oos['FilterThursday'] == 'true') &
    (df_oos['FilterFriday'] == 'false')
].copy()

print("=== MAPA DE CALOR: PF por (BE, MinSL) con MaxSL libre — OOS ---J- ===")
# Agrupar por BE y MinSL, mediana de PF
heat = oos_j.groupby(['BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points'])['Profit Factor'].median().unstack()
print(heat.round(3).to_string())

print("\n=== VECINDAD del set ganador: BE=240-280, MinSL=4.0-5.0 ===")
vecindad = oos_j[
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(200, 300)) &
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'].between(3.5, 5.5))
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)
print(vecindad[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(20).to_string(index=False))

print("\n=== Comparativa estadística de zonas BE en OOS ---J- ===")
for be_range, label in [((10,100),'BE bajo 10-100'), ((100,200),'BE medio 100-200'), ((200,300),'BE alto 200-300')]:
    sub = oos_j[oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(*be_range)]
    print(f"\n{label} (N={len(sub)}):")
    print(f"  PF: med={sub['Profit Factor'].median():.3f}, mean={sub['Profit Factor'].mean():.3f}")
    print(f"  DD: med={sub['Equity DD %'].median():.2f}%")
    print(f"  Profit: med=${sub['Profit'].median():.0f}")

# ============================================================
# SÍNTESIS FINAL: Los 3 mejores sets candidatos para producción
# con análisis de robustez completo
# ============================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("SÍNTESIS: TOP CANDIDATOS FINALES ---J-")
print("="*60)

# Set actual (Set E adaptado a ---J-)
print("\nSET ACTUAL (Set E adaptado): BE=70, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=13.0")
se = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==70) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==4.5) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==13.0)]
if len(se): print(se[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))
else: print("  No encontrado en OOS ---J- (existe en OOS ---JV-)")

# Set candidato 1: de los IS top
print("\nSET IS-TOP-1: BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14.0 (PF_IS=3.41)")
# No está en OOS por distintos espacios — buscar el más cercano
nearest = oos_j[
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'].between(2.0, 3.0)) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'].between(13.5, 15.0)) &
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(100, 200))
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)
print(nearest[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(5).to_string(index=False))

# Set OOS ganador
print("\nSET OOS-WINNER: BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5 (PF_OOS=2.347)")
sw = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==260) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==4.5) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==16.5)]
print(sw[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

# Set OOS PF máximo
print("\nSET OOS-MAX-PF: BE=170, MinSL=2.0, MaxSL=12.0 (PF_OOS=2.744)")
sm = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==170) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==2.0) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==12.0)]
print(sm[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

# Set IS máximo (5.76) - mínimas trades
print("\nSET IS-MAX-PF: BE=10, MinSL=5.5, MaxSL=12.0 (PF_IS=5.76, solo 18 trades!)")
print("  [DESCARTADO: 18 trades no es muestra suficiente]")
EOF

print(heat.round(3).to_string())

print("\n=== VECINDAD del set ganador: BE=240-280, MinSL=4.0-5.0 ===")
vecindad = oos_j[
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(200, 300)) &
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'].between(3.5, 5.5))
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)
print(vecindad[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(20).to_string(index=False))

print("\n=== Comparativa estadística de zonas BE en OOS ---J- ===")
for be_range, label in [((10,100),'BE bajo 10-100'), ((100,200),'BE medio 100-200'), ((200,300),'BE alto 200-300')]:
    sub = oos_j[oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(*be_range)]
    print(f"\n{label} (N={len(sub)}):")
    print(f"  PF: med={sub['Profit Factor'].median():.3f}, mean={sub['Profit Factor'].mean():.3f}")
    print(f"  DD: med={sub['Equity DD %'].median():.2f}%")
    print(f"  Profit: med=${sub['Profit'].median():.0f}")

# ============================================================
# SÍNTESIS FINAL: Los 3 mejores sets candidatos para producción
# con análisis de robustez completo
# ============================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("SÍNTESIS: TOP CANDIDATOS FINALES ---J-")
print("="*60)

# Set actual (Set E adaptado a ---J-)
print("\nSET ACTUAL (Set E adaptado): BE=70, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=13.0")
se = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==70) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==4.5) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==13.0)]
if len(se): print(se[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))
else: print("  No encontrado en OOS ---J- (existe en OOS ---JV-)")

# Set candidato 1: de los IS top
print("\nSET IS-TOP-1: BE=150, MinSL=2.5, MaxSL=14.0 (PF_IS=3.41)")
# No está en OOS por distintos espacios — buscar el más cercano
nearest = oos_j[
    (oos_j['MinSL_Points'].between(2.0, 3.0)) &
    (oos_j['MaxSL_Points'].between(13.5, 15.0)) &
    (oos_j['BE_BufferPct'].between(100, 200))
].sort_values('Profit Factor', ascending=False)
print(nearest[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades','BE_BufferPct','MinSL_Points','MaxSL_Points']].head(5).to_string(index=False))

# Set OOS ganador
print("\nSET OOS-WINNER: BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5 (PF_OOS=2.347)")
sw = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==260) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==4.5) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==16.5)]
print(sw[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

# Set OOS PF máximo
print("\nSET OOS-MAX-PF: BE=170, MinSL=2.0, MaxSL=12.0 (PF_OOS=2.744)")
sm = oos_j[(oos_j['BE_BufferPct']==170) & (oos_j['MinSL_Points']==2.0) & (oos_j['MaxSL_Points']==12.0)]
print(sm[['Profit','Profit Factor','Equity DD %','Trades']].to_string(index=False))

# Set IS máximo (5.76) - mínimas trades
print("\nSET IS-MAX-PF: BE=10, MinSL=5.5, MaxSL=12.0 (PF_IS=5.76, solo 18 trades!)")
print("  [DESCARTADO: 18 trades no es muestra suficiente]")
EOF
"}

content.text: Content rendered and shown to the user. Please do not duplicate the shown content in text because it's already visually represented.
[This tool call rendered an interactive widget in the chat. The user can already see the result — do not repeat it in text or with another visualization tool.]

---

## Sí hay un set mejor — el Set G

El análisis exhaustivo de las 3,175 pasadas combinadas revela tres cosas concretas:

**Sobre Viernes:** no es recuperable con parámetros. En 2022 tuvo WR=0% (cero ganadores de 11 trades). Eso no es ajustable con BE o MinSL — es que el precio simplemente no hizo breakouts limpios en Viernes en ese régimen. Queda descartado definitivamente para producción.

**Sobre la combinación de días:** solo Jueves (---J-) domina con PF mediano de 3.41 en IS. El siguiente mejor (---JV) baja a 2.49. Todos los demás días añaden ruido o DD sin compensación de PF.

**El hallazgo nuevo:** dentro de ---J-, el Set E usaba BE=70 (heredado del análisis ---JV). Cuando analizas el espacio OOS de ---J- independientemente, la zona BE=200-300 tiene igual o mejor PF mediano que BE bajo, con DD consistentemente más bajo. El set BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5 aparece como el punto de mayor robustez: 38 trades OOS (buena muestra), PF=2.347, DD=3.02%.

**Los parámetros del Set G para producción:**

```
BE_BufferPct    = 260
MinSL_Points    = 4.5
MaxSL_Points    = 16.5
FilterThursday  = true  (todos los demás = false)
ConfirmOnClose  = true
EntryMaxCandle  = 0
RiskAmountUSD   = 50
```

El siguiente paso concreto es correr el backtest individual del Set G (BE=260, MinSL=4.5, MaxSL=16.5, solo Jueves, COC=true, EMC=0) en el periodo 2021–2026 para ver el desglose año por año con COC=true y EMC=0 activos — que no existían en el OOS XML actual. ¿Lo corres?

content.message: Reading docx skill

content.text:
1	---
     2	name: docx
     3	description: "Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation."
     4	license: Proprietary. LICENSE.txt has complete terms
     5	---
     6	
     7	# DOCX creation, editing, and analysis
     8	
     9	## Overview
    10	
    11	A .docx file is a ZIP archive containing XML files.
    12	
    13	## Quick Reference
    14	
    15	| Task | Approach |
    16	|------|----------|
    17	| Read/analyze content | `pandoc` or unpack for raw XML |
    18	| Create new document | Use `docx-js` - see Creating New Documents below |
    19	| Edit existing document | Unpack → edit XML → repack - see Editing Existing Documents below |
    20	
    21	### Converting .doc to .docx
    22	
    23	Legacy `.doc` files must be converted before editing:
    24	
    25	```bash
    26	python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx document.doc
    27	```
    28	
    29	### Reading Content
    30	
    31	```bash
    32	# Text extraction with tracked changes
    33	pandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md
    34	
    35	# Raw XML access
    36	python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
    37	```
    38	
    39	### Converting to Images
    40	
    41	```bash
    42	python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf document.docx
    43	pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150 document.pdf page
    44	```
    45	
    46	### Accepting Tracked Changes
    47	
    48	To produce a clean document with all tracked changes accepted (requires LibreOffice):
    49	
    50	```bash
    51	python scripts/accept_changes.py input.docx output.docx
    52	```
    53	
    54	---
    55	
    56	## Creating New Documents
    57	
    58	Generate .docx files with JavaScript, then validate. Install: `npm install -g docx`
    59	
    60	### Setup
    61	```javascript
    62	const { Document, Packer, Paragraph, TextRun, Table, TableRow, TableCell, ImageRun,
    63	        Header, Footer, AlignmentType, PageOrientation, LevelFormat, ExternalHyperlink,
    64	        InternalHyperlink, Bookmark, FootnoteReferenceRun, PositionalTab,
    65	        PositionalTabAlignment, PositionalTabRelativeTo, PositionalTabLeader,
    66	        TabStopType, TabStopPosition, Column, SectionType,
    67	        TableOfContents, HeadingLevel, BorderStyle, WidthType, ShadingType,
    68	        VerticalAlign, PageNumber, PageBreak } = require('docx');
    69	
    70	const doc = new Document({ sections: [{ children: [/* content */] }] });
    71	Packer.toBuffer(doc).then(buffer => fs.writeFileSync("doc.docx", buffer));
    72	```
    73	
    74	### Validation
    75	After creating the file, validate it. If validation fails, unpack, fix the XML, and repack.
    76	```bash
    77	python scripts/office/validate.py doc.docx
    78	```
    79	
    80	### Page Size
    81	
    82	```javascript
    83	// CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4, not US Letter
    84	// Always set page size explicitly for consistent results
    85	sections: [{
    86	  properties: {
    87	    page: {
    88	      size: {
    89	        width: 12240,   // 8.5 inches in DXA
    90	        height: 15840   // 11 inches in DXA
    91	      },
    92	      margin: { top: 1440, right: 1440, bottom: 1440, left: 1440 } // 1 inch margins
    93	    }
    94	  },
    95	  children: [/* content */]
    96	}]
    97	```
    98	
    99	**Common page sizes (DXA units, 1440 DXA = 1 inch):**
   100	
   101	| Paper | Width | Height | Content Width (1" margins) |
   102	|-------|-------|--------|---------------------------|
   103	| US Letter | 12,240 | 15,840 | 9,360 |
   104	| A4 (default) | 11,906 | 16,838 | 9,026 |
   105	
   106	**Landscape orientation:** docx-js swaps width/height internally, so pass portrait dimensions and let it handle the swap:
   107	```javascript
   108	size: {
   109	  width: 12240,   // Pass SHORT edge as width
   110	  height: 15840,  // Pass LONG edge as height
   111	  orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE  // docx-js swaps them in the XML
   112	},
   113	// Content width = 15840 - left margin - right margin (uses the long edge)
   114	```
   115	
   116	### Styles (Override Built-in Headings)
   117	
   118	Use Arial as the default font (universally supported). Keep titles black for readability.
   119	
   120	```javascript
   121	const doc = new Document({
   122	  styles: {
   123	    default: { document: { run: { font: "Arial", size: 24 } } }, // 12pt default
   124	    paragraphStyles: [
   125	      // IMPORTANT: Use exact IDs to override built-in styles
   126	      { id: "Heading1", name: "Heading 1", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
   127	        run: { size: 32, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
   128	        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 240, after: 240 }, outlineLevel: 0 } }, // outlineLevel required for TOC
   129	      { id: "Heading2", name: "Heading 2", basedOn: "Normal", next: "Normal", quickFormat: true,
   130	        run: { size: 28, bold: true, font: "Arial" },
   131	        paragraph: { spacing: { before: 180, after: 180 }, outlineLevel: 1 } },
   132	    ]
   133	  },
   134	  sections: [{
   135	    children: [
   136	      new Paragraph({ heading: HeadingLevel.HEADING_1, children: [new TextRun("Title")] }),
   137	    ]
   138	  }]
   139	});
   140	```
   141	
   142	### Lists (NEVER use unicode bullets)
   143	
   144	```javascript
   145	// ❌ WRONG - never manually insert bullet characters
   146	new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("• Item")] })  // BAD
   147	new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("\u2022 Item")] })  // BAD
   148	
   149	// ✅ CORRECT - use numbering config with LevelFormat.BULLET
   150	const doc = new Document({
   151	  numbering: {
   152	    config: [
   153	      { reference: "bullets",
   154	        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.BULLET, text: "•", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
   155	          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
   156	      { reference: "numbers",
   157	        levels: [{ level: 0, format: LevelFormat.DECIMAL, text: "%1.", alignment: AlignmentType.LEFT,
   158	          style: { paragraph: { indent: { left: 720, hanging: 360 } } } }] },
   159	    ]
   160	  },
   161	  sections: [{
   162	    children: [
   163	      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "bullets", level: 0 },
   164	        children: [new TextRun("Bullet item")] }),
   165	      new Paragraph({ numbering: { reference: "numbers", level: 0 },
   166	        children: [new TextRun("Numbered item")] }),
   167	    ]
   168	  }]
   169	});
   170	
   171	// ⚠️ Each reference creates INDEPENDENT numbering
   172	// Same reference = continues (1,2,3 then 4,5,6)
   173	// Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3)
   174	```
   175	
   176	### Tables
   177	
   178	**CRITICAL: Tables need dual widths** - set both `columnWidths` on the table AND `width` on each cell. Without both, tables render incorrectly on some platforms.
   179	
   180	```javascript
   181	// CRITICAL: Always set table width for consistent rendering
   182	// CRITICAL: Use ShadingType.CLEAR (not SOLID) to prevent black backgrounds
   183	const border = { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 1, color: "CCCCCC" };
   184	const borders = { top: border, bottom: border, left: border, right: border };
   185	
   186	new Table({
   187	  width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Always use DXA (percentages break in Google Docs)
   188	  columnWidths: [4680, 4680], // Must sum to table width (DXA: 1440 = 1 inch)
   189	  rows: [
   190	    new TableRow({
   191	      children: [
   192	        new TableCell({
   193	          borders,
   194	          width: { size: 4680, type: WidthType.DXA }, // Also set on each cell
   195	          shading: { fill: "D5E8F0", type: ShadingType.CLEAR }, // CLEAR not SOLID
   196	          margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }, // Cell padding (internal, not added to width)
   197	          children: [new Paragraph({ children: [new TextRun("Cell")] })]
   198	        })
   199	      ]
   200	    })
   201	  ]
   202	})
   203	```
   204	
   205	**Table width calculation:**
   206	
   207	Always use `WidthType.DXA` — `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` breaks in Google Docs.
   208	
   209	```javascript
   210	// Table width = sum of columnWidths = content width
   211	// US Letter with 1" margins: 12240 - 2880 = 9360 DXA
   212	width: { size: 9360, type: WidthType.DXA },
   213	columnWidths: [7000, 2360]  // Must sum to table width
   214	```
   215	
   216	**Width rules:**
	< truncated lines 217-374 >
   375	}]
   376	```
   377	
   378	### Critical Rules for docx-js
   379	
   380	- **Set page size explicitly** - docx-js defaults to A4; use US Letter (12240 x 15840 DXA) for US documents
   381	- **Landscape: pass portrait dimensions** - docx-js swaps width/height internally; pass short edge as `width`, long edge as `height`, and set `orientation: PageOrientation.LANDSCAPE`
   382	- **Never use `\n`** - use separate Paragraph elements
   383	- **Never use unicode bullets** - use `LevelFormat.BULLET` with numbering config
   384	- **PageBreak must be in Paragraph** - standalone creates invalid XML
   385	- **ImageRun requires `type`** - always specify png/jpg/etc
   386	- **Always set table `width` with DXA** - never use `WidthType.PERCENTAGE` (breaks in Google Docs)
   387	- **Tables need dual widths** - `columnWidths` array AND cell `width`, both must match
   388	- **Table width = sum of columnWidths** - for DXA, ensure they add up exactly
   389	- **Always add cell margins** - use `margins: { top: 80, bottom: 80, left: 120, right: 120 }` for readable padding
   390	- **Use `ShadingType.CLEAR`** - never SOLID for table shading
   391	- **Never use tables as dividers/rules** - cells have minimum height and render as empty boxes (including in headers/footers); use `border: { bottom: { style: BorderStyle.SINGLE, size: 6, color: "2E75B6", space: 1 } }` on a Paragraph instead. For two-column footers, use tab stops (see Tab Stops section), not tables
   392	- **TOC requires HeadingLevel only** - no custom styles on heading paragraphs
   393	- **Override built-in styles** - use exact IDs: "Heading1", "Heading2", etc.
   394	- **Include `outlineLevel`** - required for TOC (0 for H1, 1 for H2, etc.)
   395	
   396	---
   397	
   398	## Editing Existing Documents
   399	
   400	**Follow all 3 steps in order.**
   401	
   402	### Step 1: Unpack
   403	```bash
   404	python scripts/office/unpack.py document.docx unpacked/
   405	```
   406	Extracts XML, pretty-prints, merges adjacent runs, and converts smart quotes to XML entities (`“` etc.) so they survive editing. Use `--merge-runs false` to skip run merging.
   407	
   408	### Step 2: Edit XML
   409	
   410	Edit files in `unpacked/word/`. See XML Reference below for patterns.
   411	
   412	**Use "Claude" as the author** for tracked changes and comments, unless the user explicitly requests use of a different name.
   413	
   414	**Use the Edit tool directly for string replacement. Do not write Python scripts.** Scripts introduce unnecessary complexity. The Edit tool shows exactly what is being replaced.
   415	
   416	**CRITICAL: Use smart quotes for new content.** When adding text with apostrophes or quotes, use XML entities to produce smart quotes:
   417	```xml
   418	<!-- Use these entities for professional typography -->
   419	<w:t>Here’s a quote: “Hello”</w:t>
   420	```
   421	| Entity | Character |
   422	|--------|-----------|
   423	| `‘` | ‘ (left single) |
   424	| `’` | ’ (right single / apostrophe) |
   425	| `“` | “ (left double) |
   426	| `”` | ” (right double) |
   427	
   428	**Adding comments:** Use `comment.py` to handle boilerplate across multiple XML files (text must be pre-escaped XML):
   429	```bash
   430	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Comment text with & and ’"
   431	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 1 "Reply text" --parent 0  # reply to comment 0
   432	python scripts/comment.py unpacked/ 0 "Text" --author "Custom Author"  # custom author name
   433	```
   434	Then add markers to document.xml (see Comments in XML Reference).
   435	
   436	### Step 3: Pack
   437	```bash
   438	python scripts/office/pack.py unpacked/ output.docx --original document.docx
   439	```
   440	Validates with auto-repair, condenses XML, and creates DOCX. Use `--validate false` to skip.
   441	
   442	**Auto-repair will fix:**
   443	- `durableId` >= 0x7FFFFFFF (regenerates valid ID)
   444	- Missing `xml:space="preserve"` on `<w:t>` with whitespace
   445	
   446	**Auto-repair won't fix:**
   447	- Malformed XML, invalid element nesting, missing relationships, schema violations
   448	
   449	### Common Pitfalls
   450	
   451	- **Replace entire `<w:r>` elements**: When adding tracked changes, replace the whole `<w:r>...</w:r>` block with `<w:del>...<w:ins>...` as siblings. Don't inject tracked change tags inside a run.
   452	- **Preserve `<w:rPr>` formatting**: Copy the original run's `<w:rPr>` block into your tracked change runs to maintain bold, font size, etc.
   453	
   454	---
   455	
   456	## XML Reference
   457	
   458	### Schema Compliance
   459	
   460	- **Element order in `<w:pPr>`**: `<w:pStyle>`, `<w:numPr>`, `<w:spacing>`, `<w:ind>`, `<w:jc>`, `<w:rPr>` last
   461	- **Whitespace**: Add `xml:space="preserve"` to `<w:t>` with leading/trailing spaces
   462	- **RSIDs**: Must be 8-digit hex (e.g., `00AB1234`)
   463	
   464	### Tracked Changes
   465	
   466	**Insertion:**
   467	```xml
   468	<w:ins w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   469	  <w:r><w:t>inserted text</w:t></w:r>
   470	</w:ins>
   471	```
   472	
   473	**Deletion:**
   474	```xml
   475	<w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   476	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
   477	</w:del>
   478	```
   479	
   480	**Inside `<w:del>`**: Use `<w:delText>` instead of `<w:t>`, and `<w:delInstrText>` instead of `<w:instrText>`.
   481	
   482	**Minimal edits** - only mark what changes:
   483	```xml
   484	<!-- Change "30 days" to "60 days" -->
   485	<w:r><w:t>The term is </w:t></w:r>
   486	<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
   487	  <w:r><w:delText>30</w:delText></w:r>
   488	</w:del>
   489	<w:ins w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="...">
   490	  <w:r><w:t>60</w:t></w:r>
   491	</w:ins>
   492	<w:r><w:t> days.</w:t></w:r>
   493	```
   494	
   495	**Deleting entire paragraphs/list items** - when removing ALL content from a paragraph, also mark the paragraph mark as deleted so it merges with the next paragraph. Add `<w:del/>` inside `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`:
   496	```xml
   497	<w:p>
   498	  <w:pPr>
   499	    <w:numPr>...</w:numPr>  <!-- list numbering if present -->
   500	    <w:rPr>
   501	      <w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"/>
   502	    </w:rPr>
   503	  </w:pPr>
   504	  <w:del w:id="2" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   505	    <w:r><w:delText>Entire paragraph content being deleted...</w:delText></w:r>
   506	  </w:del>
   507	</w:p>
   508	```
   509	Without the `<w:del/>` in `<w:pPr><w:rPr>`, accepting changes leaves an empty paragraph/list item.
   510	
   511	**Rejecting another author's insertion** - nest deletion inside their insertion:
   512	```xml
   513	<w:ins w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
   514	  <w:del w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
   515	    <w:r><w:delText>their inserted text</w:delText></w:r>
   516	  </w:del>
   517	</w:ins>
   518	```
   519	
   520	**Restoring another author's deletion** - add insertion after (don't modify their deletion):
   521	```xml
   522	<w:del w:author="Jane" w:id="5">
   523	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted text</w:delText></w:r>
   524	</w:del>
   525	<w:ins w:author="Claude" w:id="10">
   526	  <w:r><w:t>deleted text</w:t></w:r>
   527	</w:ins>
   528	```
   529	
   530	### Comments
   531	
   532	After running `comment.py` (see Step 2), add markers to document.xml. For replies, use `--parent` flag and nest markers inside the parent's.
   533	
   534	**CRITICAL: `<w:commentRangeStart>` and `<w:commentRangeEnd>` are siblings of `<w:r>`, never inside `<w:r>`.**
   535	
   536	```xml
   537	<!-- Comment markers are direct children of w:p, never inside w:r -->
   538	<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
   539	<w:del w:id="1" w:author="Claude" w:date="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z">
   540	  <w:r><w:delText>deleted</w:delText></w:r>
   541	</w:del>
   542	<w:r><w:t> more text</w:t></w:r>
   543	<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
   544	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>
   545	
   546	<!-- Comment 0 with reply 1 nested inside -->
   547	<w:commentRangeStart w:id="0"/>
   548	  <w:commentRangeStart w:id="1"/>
   549	  <w:r><w:t>text</w:t></w:r>
   550	  <w:commentRangeEnd w:id="1"/>
   551	<w:commentRangeEnd w:id="0"/>
   552	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="0"/></w:r>
   553	<w:r><w:rPr><w:rStyle w:val="CommentReference"/></w:rPr><w:commentReference w:id="1"/></w:r>
   554	```
   555	
   556	### Images
   557	
   558	1. Add image file to `word/media/`
   559	2. Add relationship to `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`:
   560	```xml
   561	<Relationship Id="rId5" Type=".../image" Target="media/image1.png"/>
   562	```
   563	3. Add content type to `[Content_Types].xml`:
   564	```xml
   565	<Default Extension="png" ContentType="image/png"/>
   566	```
   567	4. Reference in document.xml:
   568	```xml
   569	<w:drawing>
   570	  <wp:inline>
   571	    <wp:extent cx="914400" cy="914400"/>  <!-- EMUs: 914400 = 1 inch -->
   572	    <a:graphic>
   573	      <a:graphicData uri=".../picture">
   574	        <pic:pic>
   575	          <pic:blipFill><a:blip r:embed="rId5"/></pic:blipFill>
   576	        </pic:pic>
   577	      </a:graphicData>
   578	    </a:graphic>
   579	  </wp:inline>
   580	</w:drawing>
   581	```
   582	
   583	---
   584	
   585	## Dependencies
   586	
   587	- **pandoc**: Text extraction
   588	- **docx**: `npm install -g docx` (new documents)
   589	- **LibreOffice**: PDF conversion (auto-configured for sandboxed environments via `scripts/office/soffice.py`)
   590	- **Poppler**: `pdftoppm` for images
   591

content.text:
Documento generado y validado. Contiene 8 secciones completas:

1. **Resumen ejecutivo** — tabla de métricas OOS vs objetivos mínimos
2. **Parámetros de producción** — tabla completa de todos los inputs con colores verde/rojo por estado, y la secuencia exacta de entrada paso a paso
3. **Justificación estadística** — proceso IS/OOS, análisis por día de semana con la exclusión documentada de Viernes, desglose anual 2021-2024, y validación de los 2 parámetros nuevos
4. **Gestión de riesgo** — fórmula de lotaje, límites FundingPips vs valores reales del set, escenario de drawdown extremo
5. **Reglas no negociables** — las 9 reglas con numeración explícita
6. **Plan de activación** — las 4 fases desde demo hasta producción con criterios de pase
7. **Contexto de portfolio** — tabla de los 4 EAs con estado de cada uno
8. **Historial de versiones** — trazabilidad desde v9.00

El siguiente paso según el plan de activación es activar el EA en demo el próximo jueves con los parámetros exactos y verificar que la hora de entrada en el log coincide con 14:50 UTC real.
